content.text: Hypercare Team Staffing and Coverage Model

Hypercare is the elevated operational posture maintained for the first 30 days after a Claude API feature or model release, bridging the heightened Day-1 launch window and the steady-state Day-2 operations model. It is staffed by three tiers of personnel whose involvement tapers at different rates: feature engineers remain intensively active for the first week, then shift to on-call-only; SRE maintains continuous coverage throughout; customer support ramps up to handle customer questions and stabilizes.

Hypercare is not Day-1 repeated for 30 days. It is a structured wind-down from launch intensity to normal operations. Each tier has a defined exit condition — when it is met, that tier's involvement decreases. Hypercare ends not on day 30 by calendar, but when all exit conditions are satisfied.

This sub-subtopic covers the three-tier staffing model, coverage schedules per phase, exit conditions for each tier, the on-call rotation design for hypercare, escalation paths, tooling for tracking hypercare state, and common staffing anti-patterns.

attrs.alt: 3-tier hypercare staffing over 30 days: Feature Engineers taper from daily to on-call; SRE constant; Support ramps then stabilizes

content.text: Feature engineers are the people who built and launched the feature. They have the deepest context on behavior, edge cases, and safe rollback paths. Their hypercare obligation is highest in the first week and decreases as operational knowledge transfers to SRE.
Daily standup, dashboard review, 5-min response to SRE pings
Zero P0 incidents for 48h; SRE can diagnose P1s independently
Every-other-day check-in; respond to Slack within 2h
SRE handles two P1 incidents without feature-engineer involvement
No P0s for 7 days; support ticket volume stable for 5 days

SRE provides 24/7 coverage throughout the full hypercare period. Their obligation does not taper in terms of availability — what changes is their autonomy: in week 1 they escalate frequently to feature engineers; by week 3 they handle most incidents independently.
Primary incident responder + first alert receiver; escalates P1+ to feature engineers within 10 min
Any P1 or higher → escalate to feature engineer immediately
Primary for all severities; feature engineer secondary
P1 not resolved in 30 min → escalate; P0 → escalate immediately
Primary + secondary for most incidents; feature engineer tertiary
P0 only → escalate to feature engineer; P1/P2 → SRE handles independently

Customer support ramps up on Day 1 as customers encounter the new behavior, handles a burst of questions in the first few days, then stabilizes. Their hypercare obligation is principally responsiveness and routing accuracy.
High volume: questions about behavior changes, new features, migration
Any reproducible bug with customer impact → P2 ticket to engineering
Moderate volume: edge cases, documentation gaps
Unresolved after 24h or customer-reported data issue → escalate
Normal volume; FAQ now covers most questions
Only confirmed bugs → engineering; behavior questions → product docs

The coverage schedule defines which engineers are on-call at what times and at which tier. For a global API like Claude, follow-the-sun coverage eliminates the need for any engineer to be paged outside business hours.
EU feature engineer on-call (if available) or async US backup
Async US backup; APAC SRE primary for Tier 2

# setup_hypercare_oncall.py — create hypercare PagerDuty schedule via API
import requests, os
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

PD_KEY = os.environ["PAGERDUTY_API_KEY"]
HEADERS = {
    "Authorization": f"Token token={PD_KEY}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    "Accept": "application/vnd.pagerduty+json;version=2",
}

def create_hypercare_schedule(
    feature_name: str,
    tier1_engineers: list[str],   # list of PagerDuty user IDs
    sre_ids: list[str],
    start_date: str               # ISO date string "2024-04-15"
):
    """Create a 30-day hypercare schedule with escalation layers."""
    schedule = {
        "schedule": {
            "name": f"Hypercare: {feature_name}",
            "type": "schedule",
            "time_zone": "UTC",
            "description": f"30-day hypercare on-call for {feature_name}",
            "schedule_layers": [
                {
                    "name": "Tier-1 Feature Engineers (Days 1-7)",
                    "start": f"{start_date}T00:00:00Z",
                    "end": None,
                    "rotation_type": "weekly",
                    "rotation_virtual_start": f"{start_date}T00:00:00Z",
                    "users": [{"user": {"id": uid, "type": "user_reference"}} for uid in tier1_engineers],
                    "restrictions": [
                        # Business hours only for Tier 1 (SRE covers off-hours)
                        {"type": "weekly_restriction", "start_day_of_week": 1,
                         "start_time_of_day": "09:00:00", "duration_seconds": 36000}
                    ]
                },
                {
                    "name": "SRE 24/7 (Full 30 days)",
                    "start": f"{start_date}T00:00:00Z",
                    "end": None,
                    "rotation_type": "daily",
                    "rotation_virtual_start": f"{start_date}T00:00:00Z",
                    "users": [{"user": {"id": uid, "type": "user_reference"}} for uid in sre_ids],
                }
            ]
        }
    }
    r = requests.post("https://api.pagerduty.com/schedules", json=schedule, headers=HEADERS)
    r.raise_for_status()
    schedule_id = r.json()["schedule"]["id"]
    print(f"Created hypercare schedule: {schedule_id}")
    return schedule_id

create_hypercare_schedule(
    feature_name="claude-3-7-sonnet-ga",
    tier1_engineers=["P1A2B3C", "P4D5E6F"],
    sre_ids=["P7G8H9I", "P0J1K2L", "P3M4N5O"],
    start_date="2024-04-15"
)
Hypercare phases exit on evidence, not calendar dates. The Phase Exit Criteria (PEC) for each transition must all be satisfied before the next phase begins.
Zero P0 incidents in the previous 48 hours
SRE has independently diagnosed at least one P1 incident without feature-engineer involvement
Support ticket volume has peaked and is trending downward for 2 consecutive days
All known issues from the Day-1 window have either been resolved or have an accepted workaround and ETA
Feature engineer has delivered the operations brief and SRE has confirmed understanding
SRE has handled two or more P1 incidents independently (no escalation to feature engineer)
Support FAQ covers the top 5 customer questions observed in the first week
No new P0 incidents in 7 days
Alert thresholds have been reviewed and any Day-1 tightening has been reverted or formalized
Runbook updates from the intensive phase are merged and scored ≥ 80
Support ticket volume at or below pre-launch baseline for 5 consecutive days
Feature engineer has been removed from primary on-call; SRE handles all tiers
Retrospective completed and action items assigned
Documentation (API reference, changelog, migration guide) reviewed and final
SRE → Feature Eng (10 min) → EM (20 min) → VP Eng (30 min)
SRE handles; feature eng available same-day
SRE → Feature Eng (15 min) → EM (30 min)
SRE handles; Feature Eng at 30 min if not resolved
SRE → Feature Eng (30 min) → EM (60 min)
SRE → normal escalation path (no feature-eng guarantee)
Feature engineer as sole hypercare owner
Burnout in week 1; knowledge silo prevents SRE from ever being autonomous
Tier 2 SRE is primary from Day 1; feature engineer is secondary
Hypercare treated as calendar event (30 days regardless)
Exit before SRE is autonomous, or stay in intensive mode unnecessarily
Exit on evidence (Phase Exit Criteria), not calendar
No formal handoff between intensive and transition phases
Feature engineers keep getting paged in transition phase because SRE doesn't know they're supposed to be primary
Explicit phase announcement in Slack; PagerDuty rotation updated on phase change
Customer support not included in hypercare
Support escalates every unusual question to engineering, creating interruption load
Support brief delivered Day 1; support team attends daily standup in intensive phase
One engineer cannot provide 24/7 coverage; vacations and illness leave gaps
Minimum 2 feature engineers in Tier 1; at least one per timezone shift
Hypercare ends abruptly without documentation update
Runbooks and dashboards reflect hypercare configuration; SRE inherits mis-tuned alerts
Documentation and alert-threshold normalization is a mandatory exit criterion
A single dashboard showing the current hypercare phase, which exit criteria are met, and who is on-call gives all stakeholders visibility without requiring a Slack message to find out.

# hypercare_dashboard.py — render hypercare state as a Slack Block Kit message
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def render_hypercare_status(
    feature: str,
    launch_date: datetime,
    current_phase: str,
    exit_criteria: dict,
    oncall: dict
) -> dict:
    days_since_launch = (datetime.utcnow() - launch_date).days

    criteria_lines = []
    for criterion, met in exit_criteria.items():
        icon = ":white_check_mark:" if met else ":x:"
        criteria_lines.append(f"{icon} {criterion}")

    return {
        "blocks": [
            {"type": "header", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": f"Hypercare Status: {feature}"}},
            {"type": "section", "fields": [
                {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": f"*Phase:* {current_phase}"},
                {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": f"*Day:* {days_since_launch} of 30"},
                {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": f"*Tier-1 on-call:* {oncall.get('tier1', 'None')}"},
                {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": f"*SRE on-call:* {oncall.get('sre', 'Unknown')}"},
            ]},
            {"type": "section", "text": {
                "type": "mrkdwn",
                "text": "*Phase Exit Criteria:*\n" + "\n".join(criteria_lines)
            }},
        ]
    }

# Example usage — post to #hypercare-[feature] channel
status = render_hypercare_status(
    feature="claude-3-7-sonnet",
    launch_date=datetime(2024, 4, 15),
    current_phase="Intensive",
    exit_criteria={
        "Zero P0 incidents in 48h": True,
        "SRE handled P1 independently": False,
        "Support volume declining": True,
        "Known issues resolved/ETA": False,
    },
    oncall={"tier1": "alice@", "sre": "carol@"}
)
import json
print(json.dumps(status, indent=2))

The hypercare staffing model uses three tiers — feature engineers, SRE, and customer support — whose intensity tapers at different rates over 30 days. Feature engineers are daily-active in week 1 and on-call-only by week 3; SRE provides uninterrupted 24/7 coverage throughout; support ramps up and stabilizes. Phases exit on evidence (Phase Exit Criteria), not calendar. PagerDuty follow-the-sun rotation eliminates off-hours paging for any single region. The most important anti-patterns to avoid are making a single engineer the sole owner, treating hypercare as a calendar event, and skipping the formal phase transition announcement that updates the escalation path.
Tier 1 (Feature Eng): daily-active Day 1–7, every-other-day Day 7–14, weekly sync Day 14–30
Tier 2 (SRE): 24/7 primary throughout all 30 days; autonomy increases per phase
Tier 3 (Support): briefed Day 1; ramp-up burst Day 1–3; stable coverage Day 3–30
Phase exit on evidence: zero P0s, SRE independent P1 resolution, support volume stable
Follow-the-sun PagerDuty rotation ensures coverage across Americas/EMEA/APAC
Exit criteria dashboard (Slack Block Kit) gives real-time phase status to all stakeholders
